class.name: EFSWWatcher
class.brief_description: A wrapper class for FileWatcher in [url=https://github.com/SpartanJ/efsw/]efsw[/url].
class.description:
Listens to files and directories and dispatches events to notify the listener of file and directory changes. Here is a simple example: [codeblock] extends Node
const ACTION_NAMES = ["Add", "Delete", "Modified", "Moved"]
var listener : EFSWListener var watcher : EFSWWatcher
# A simple function for handling file actions. func _file_action_handle(watch_id: int, directory: String, filename: String, is_dir: bool, action: EFSWListener.FileAction, old_filename: String) -> void: # Simply output the detected file actions. prints("watch id: %d, dir: %s filename: %s, is dir: %s, action: %s, old_filename: %s" % [watch_id, directory, filename, is_dir, ACTION_NAMES[action-1], old_filename])
func _enter_tree() -> void: listener = EFSWListener.new() watcher = EFSWWatcher.new()
listener.set_file_action_handler(_file_action_handle) # Sets the callable used to handle file actions. watcher.add_watch("/opt/godot/projects", listener, false) # Adds directory to watch. watcher.watch() # Starts watching.
func _exit_tree() -> void: listener.free() watcher.free() [/codeblock] [b]Note:[/b] An OS-specific backend implementation is used by default; if no specific backend implementation exists for the current OS platform, a generic backend implementation will be used instead. [b]Note:[/b] When used on remote file systems, you may need to enable [member force_generic] to use the generic backend.

method.name: add_watch
method.description: Adds a watch for [param directory]. On success, returns a positive [int] that can be used in [method remove_watch_by_id]; otherwise, returns an [enum WatcherError]. If [param recursive] is [code]true[/code], the subdirectories within the [param directory] will be automatically watched recursively. File change events in the directory will be listened to and handled by [param listener]. [b]Note:[/b] For directories where watches are added automatically, it is still possible to add watches manually.

method.name: get_watch_directories
method.description: Returns a list of directories that were manually added for watching.

method.name: remove_watch_by_id
method.description: Removes the directory watch specified by [param watch_id], which was previously returned by [method add_watch].

method.name: watch
method.description: Starts watching in a separate thread.

members.member: Whether to allow symlinks to watch recursively out of the pointed directory. [member follow_symlinks] must be enabled for this to work. [b]Note:[/b] Enabling this may lead to infinite recursion and cause crash.
Whether to allow recursive watchers to follow symbolic links to other directories.

Whether to force the use of a generic backend implementation. Usefully for remote file systems. [b]Note:[/b] Toggling this value will release the backend instance and all configurations will be reset.

constants.constant: The directory to be watched does not exist or is inaccessible.
The directory to be watched has already been manually added for watching.
The directory to be watched is a symbolic link pointing outside its base directory when [member follow_symlinks] is disabled.
The directory to be watched lacks read permissions.
The directory to be watched is in a remote file system. Please use [EFSWWatcher] with [member force_generic] enabled instead.
File system watcher failed to watch for changes.
